Daily US-Israel-Iran War Brief:
16th Jun 2026:

- We will be posting daily war updates on the ongoing war in Middle East
- ⁠Follow us for daily Brief

● The signing of the MoU between the US and Iran is scheduled for Friday, 19 June, at the Bürgenstock resort in central Switzerland.

● The maritime blockade has begun to be lifted operationally. Iranian officials reported that three oil tankers and two vessels carrying essential goods have successfully crossed through previously restricted waters in the Strait of Hormuz.

● Qatar announced plans to rapidly increase LNG production once the Strait of Hormuz fully reopens, aiming to restore most of its export capacity within two months.

● President Trump said the Iran deal “was done” and would now move into a second stage.

● Israel requested access to the full text of the US-Iran MoU but was denied.

● Iranian Parliament Speaker Mohammad Bagher Ghalibaf told his Lebanese counterpart Nabih Berri that Israel must withdraw from all occupied areas in Lebanon.

tweets.title: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy,  Prime Minister Narendra Modi meet on the sidelines of the G7 Summit in Évian, France; focus on joint projects
tweets.content:
Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy, Prime Minister Narendra Modi meet on the sidelines of the G7 Summit in Évian, France; focus on joint projects

Volodymyr Zelenskyy / Володимир Зеленський (@ZelenskyyUa)

tweets.title:
A scramjet cannot operate from a standstill. It first needs a powerful booster to accelerate the vehicle to very high supersonic/hypersonic speeds before the scramjet can ignite.

That is why HSTDV was mounted on an Agni-derived booster, which accelerated the vehicle to the required speed and altitude before the scramjet took over. The same principle is used in other hypersonic programmes worldwide, including Russia’s Zircon, where a rocket booster brings the missile into the scramjet’s operating envelope.

The concept of encapsulating a scramjet vehicle inside a booster and triggering the engine only after the required speed and flight conditions are achieved is not easy to grasp for those new to defence technology.

Image @Kuntal__biswas : Thank you

A major multi-state investigation is unfolding after security agencies expanded their probe into an alleged network linked to Pakistan-based handlers. The latest development came with the detention of a suspect from Rajasthan's Bhiwadi region, whose name reportedly surfaced during the interrogation of individuals arrested earlier by the Madhya Pradesh ATS.

Investigators are examining a complex web of digital communications, including Telegram and WhatsApp groups, suspected foreign contacts, financial transactions, and possible links stretching across Rajasthan, Haryana, Madhya Pradesh, and Uttar Pradesh. Authorities are also analyzing seized mobile phones, chat records, PDF documents, and other electronic evidence as part of a broader forensic investigation.

According to officials, the case began gaining momentum after the arrest of a suspect in Bhopal, which led investigators to additional individuals and locations. The probe has since expanded to examine alleged connections with Pakistan-based handlers, possible radicalization activities, digital networking, and the role of encrypted communication platforms.

Multiple agencies, including ATS units, intelligence officials, and state police forces, are now working together to determine the full extent of the network. Investigators are also exploring the possibility of foreign funding channels and whether additional individuals across different states may have been involved.

As the investigation progresses, security agencies remain focused on uncovering the complete picture behind the alleged network and identifying any potential threats to national security. Further revelations are expected as forensic analysis and interrogations continue.

BREAKING: Telegram access in India will be temporarily restricted ahead of NEET Re-Exam

The National Testing Agency (NTA) says Telegram access in India will be temporarily restricted until 22 June 2026, while the platform's message-editing feature will reportedly remain disabled until 30 June 2026.

According to NTA, the measures are aimed at curbing exam-related fraud, fake paper leak claims, and misinformation ahead of the NEET (UG) 2026 re-examination on 21 June.

Authorities say multiple Telegram channels, groups, and bots linked to alleged exam scams have already been taken down.

INDIA PUSHES BACK ON UNSC REFORM DRAFT

India has sharply criticized the latest UN Security Council reform document, calling it "subjective" and inaccurate in reflecting member states' positions.

India's UN envoy Harish Parvathaneni demanded a shift to formal text-based negotiations with clear timelines, arguing that a majority of UN members support expanding permanent membership.

New Delhi also rejected proposals it says would dilute genuine permanent representation and further delay long-pending UNSC reforms.

🇺🇸 A U.S. Air Force B-52H Stratofortress (60-061), assigned to the 412th Test Wing at Edwards AFB, reportedly crashed shortly after takeoff during a radar test mission.

The aircraft, modified to test the new AN/APQ-188 AESA radar, lost control and crashed back onto the runway. All eight personnel on board, including USAF members and civilian contractors, were reported killed.

🚨 REPORT: A proposed U.S.–Iran agreement could eventually open the door to as much as $300 billion in Gulf-funded investment for Iran's reconstruction, according to U.S. officials.

The reported framework would also include:
• Phased sanctions relief
• Access to frozen Iranian assets
• Reopening of the Strait of Hormuz
• Nuclear compliance and inspection requirements

Any economic benefits would reportedly be conditional on Iran meeting agreed obligations, though the full text of the agreement has not yet been released.
